content:
# elizaos/spartan Weekly Report (Feb 15 - 21, 2026)

## 🚀 Highlights
This week, development on the Spartan project focused on stabilizing the environment and ensuring seamless integration with the broader ElizaOS ecosystem. The primary theme of the week was infrastructure refinement, specifically addressing build consistency and cross-version compatibility. By implementing a dedicated compatibility layer, the team is laying the groundwork for more modular and extensible agent deployments across different environments.

## 🛠️️ Key Developments

### Core Compatibility & Infrastructure Stability
The week's technical efforts were centered on resolving friction between the staging environment and the core framework.
*   **Core-Compat Layer:** A significant effort was initiated to implement a compatibility layer designed to bridge service gaps and ensure the Spartan framework remains aligned with core ElizaOS updates.
*   **Staging & Build Fixes:** Work was completed to resolve persistent service and build issues that were impacting the staging environment. These fixes streamline the development pipeline and reduce deployment friction.
*   **Impactful PRs:** [#24](https://github.com/elizaos/spartan/pull/24) served as the primary vehicle for these improvements, bundling staging fixes with the new core-compat layer.

## 🐛 Issues & Triage

### New & Active Issues
The project is currently focused on the integration of the core-compat layer. While no new standalone issues were reported in the daily data, the activity surrounding the resolution of service/build issues indicates a high priority on maintaining a stable CI/CD pipeline. The ongoing work in [#24](https://github.com/elizaos/spartan/pull/24) remains the central point of technical focus for the team.

## 💬 Community & Collaboration
Collaboration this week was characterized by targeted infrastructure improvements. The opening of PR [#24](https://github.com/elizaos/spartan/pull/24) suggests active maintenance aimed at resolving blockers that affect the wider contributor base's ability to build and test services. The focus on "core-compat" reflects a commitment to the project's philosophy of modularity, ensuring that Spartan remains a functional and integrated component of the larger ElizaOS open-source ecosystem.
